title: vicia gemella subsp. pubescens
intro: Vicia gemella subsp. pubescens, also known as twin vetch, is a perennial plant that belongs to the legume family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Twin vetch can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers are often used fresh or dried for herbal remedies.
